_meta.note: vitality_aes = Glottolog AES (açık); EGIDS/UNESCO/ElCat eşlemesi crosswalk'ta.

vitality_aes.label: threatened
vitality_aes.comment: Khalaj (4396-klj) = Vulnerable (20 percent certain, based on the evidence available) [Knüppel 2010](cldf:knueppel:10)

vitality_aes.label: not endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Uyghur (uig-uig) = 2 (Provincial). Language of recognized nationality: Uygur.

vitality_aes.label: threatened
vitality_aes.comment: Bashkir (334-bak) = Vulnerable

vitality_aes.label: threatened
vitality_aes.comment: Crimean Tatar (10423-crh) = Vulnerable (60 percent certain, based on the evidence available) (In some communities, children learn the language, but many stop using it in adulthood. Probably all speakers exhibit strong influence of related Turkic languages, Russian or Bulgarian, and younger people may be less competent in Crimean Tatar. Definitely endangered.) [Salminen 2007](cldf:salminen:07:2)

vitality_aes.label: not endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Karakalpak (kaa-kaa) = 5* (Developing). Statutory provincial language in Karakalpakstan Autonomous Republic (1993, Constitution of the Republic of Karakalpakstan, Part I, Chapter 1, Article 4).

vitality_aes.label: threatened
vitality_aes.comment: Karachay-Balkar (1075-krc) = Vulnerable

vitality_aes.label: not endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Kazakh (kaz-kaz) = 1 (National). Statutory national language (1995, Constitution, Article 7(1)).

vitality_aes.label: not endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Kyrgyz (kir-kir) = 1 (National). Statutory national language (2010, Constitution, Art 5 (1)).

vitality_aes.label: threatened
vitality_aes.comment: Kumyk (1074-kum) = Vulnerable

vitality_aes.label: shifting
vitality_aes.crosswalk: EGIDS: 7; UNESCO: definitely endangered; ElCat: definitely endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Noghay (2897-nog) = Threatened (80 percent certain, based on the evidence available) [Salminen 2007](cldf:salminen:07:2)

vitality_aes.label: not endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Tatar (tat-tat) = 2 (Provincial). Statutory provincial language in Tatarstan Republic (1992, Constitution of the Republic of Tatarstan, Article 8).

vitality_aes.label: threatened
vitality_aes.comment: Chuvash (338-chv) = Vulnerable

vitality_aes.label: threatened
vitality_aes.comment: Gagauz (3406-gag) = Vulnerable (80 percent certain, based on the evidence available) [Salminen 2007](cldf:salminen:07:2)

vitality_aes.label: not endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Azerbaijani, North (azj-azj) = 1 (National). Statutory national language (1995, Constitution, Article 21(1)).

vitality_aes.label: not endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Turkish (tur-tur) = 1 (National). Statutory national language (1982, Constitution, Article 3).

vitality_aes.label: not endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Turkmen (tuk-tuk) = 1 (National). Statutory national language (1992, Constitution, Article 13).

vitality_aes.label: shifting
vitality_aes.crosswalk: EGIDS: 7; UNESCO: definitely endangered; ElCat: definitely endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Dolgan (2213-dlg) = Threatened (60 percent certain, based on the evidence available) [Salminen 2007](cldf:salminen:07:1)

vitality_aes.label: shifting
vitality_aes.crosswalk: EGIDS: 7; UNESCO: definitely endangered; ElCat: definitely endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Khakas (2434-kjh) = Threatened (80 percent certain, based on the evidence available) [Salminen 2007](cldf:salminen:07:2)

vitality_aes.label: moribund
vitality_aes.crosswalk: EGIDS: 8a; UNESCO: severely endangered; ElCat: severely endangered
vitality_aes.comment:
Shor (3048-cjs) = Severely Endangered (80 percent certain, based on the evidence available) (There are probably no child speakers, except possibly in a few rural families,
and most speakers are middle-aged or older.) [Salminen 2007](cldf:salminen:07:2)

vitality_aes.label: threatened
vitality_aes.comment: Yakut (10454-sah) = Vulnerable (60 percent certain, based on the evidence available) (approximately 93% of Sakha know their heritage language) [Pakendorf 2007](cldf:pakendorf:07)

vitality_aes.label: shifting
vitality_aes.crosswalk: EGIDS: 7; UNESCO: definitely endangered; ElCat: definitely endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Southern Altai (3044-alt) = Threatened (80 percent certain, based on the evidence available) [Salminen 2000](cldf:salminen:00)

vitality_aes.label: not endangered
vitality_aes.comment: Tuva (7309-tyv) = At risk (20 percent certain, based on the evidence available) [ed. 2010](cldf:ed:10)
